context: Tests whether the agent produces a code review that identifies security vulnerabilities (SQL injection, leaked secret), uses blocking comment format correctly, references line numbers, and leads with positives before raising issues.

checklist.name: Leads with positives
checklist.description: The review opens by acknowledging something that works well or is done correctly before raising any issues

checklist.name: SQL injection identified
checklist.description: The f-string SQL query (get_user function) is flagged as a SQL injection vulnerability

checklist.name: SQL injection is blocking
checklist.description: The SQL injection issue is labeled as a blocking issue (not a nit or suggestion)

checklist.name: Hardcoded secret identified
checklist.description: The hardcoded API_KEY value is flagged as a leaked secret or credential exposure problem

checklist.name: Secret is blocking
checklist.description: The hardcoded secret is labeled as a blocking issue

checklist.name: Blocking comment explains consequence
checklist.description: At least one blocking comment includes both what the problem is AND what the consequence/risk is (e.g. 'will throw', 'allows attacker to', 'exposes credential')

checklist.name: Blocking comment suggests resolution
checklist.description: At least one blocking comment includes a concrete suggestion or fix direction

checklist.name: Line numbers referenced
checklist.description: At least one comment references a specific line number from the input file

checklist.name: Missing 404 handling identified
checklist.description: The get_user function's lack of handling when fetchone() returns None (user not found) is flagged

checklist.name: One comment per concern
checklist.description: SQL injection and hardcoded secret are raised as separate comments/sections, not bundled into one
